mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns exhibit a rank correlation with INDPRO changes below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.75%, the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows rises sufficiently to depress valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances or the rank information coefficient remains above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales alongside elevated short-term interest rates creates a joint drag on consumer demand and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS yields a rank information coefficient above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment already low, a rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals impending economic contraction and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days as recession risk is repriced.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points in two months
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of occurrences, or the rank information coefficient remains above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly captures aggregate demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns show a rank correlation with GDPC1 growth below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating consumer price inflation and a tight labor market raises the probability of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized 3-month change exceeds 2.5% and UNRATE is below 4.5%
regime_hint: tight labor market with rising inflation
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months or achieves a rank information coefficient above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in residential construction activity and forward-looking demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes exhibit positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the rank information coefficient remains above zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries with elevated unemployment, monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200 thousand indicate accelerating labor market healing that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the rank information coefficient falls below zero.
